prompt_text:
You are shown a SKELETON view of a java repository: class headers,
inheritance, method signatures, and attributes — but NO method bodies.

Use this signature-level view to detect structural violations of Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP):
- Fat interfaces / bloated classes (many unrelated method groups)
- Clients forced to implement methods they don't need (abstract methods / NotImplementedError / empty overrides)
- Inheritance hierarchies where subclasses alter the contract shape
- Broad API...
raw_response:
```json
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/ByteSourceJsonBootstrapper.java",
      "entity_name": "ByteSourceJsonBootstrapper.constructParser",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 283,
      "line_end": 300,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Direct instantiation of concrete parser implementation",
      "reasoning": "The constructParser method directly instantiates UTF8StreamJsonParser (L299). This couples the bootstrapping logic to a specific parser implementation rather than depending on an abstraction or using a factory/injection mechanism."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/base/GeneratorBase.java",
      "entity_name": "GeneratorBase._constructDefaultPrettyPrinter",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 377,
      "line_end": 379,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Abstract base class hard-codes dependency on concrete PrettyPrinter",
      "reasoning": "The abstract class GeneratorBase directly instantiates DefaultPrettyPrinter (L378). Abstractions should not depend on details; hard-coding a specific implementation of an interface in a base class prevents users from easily substituting different defaults."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/io/IOContext.java",
      "entity_name": "IOContext.constructTextBuffer",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 214,
      "line_end": 217,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Context object directly instantiates concrete buffer implementation",
      "reasoning": "IOContext directly instantiates TextBuffer (L216) and ReadConstrainedTextBuffer (L220). This creates a tight coupling between the I/O context and specific buffer implementations, making it difficult to swap buffer management strategies."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/base/TextualTSFactory.java",
      "entity_name": "TextualTSFactory._createWriter",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 339,
      "line_end": 346,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Base factory hard-codes dependency on concrete Writer implementation",
      "reasoning": "TextualTSFactory, which serves as a high-level factory abstraction, directly instantiates UTF8Writer (L345) instead of delegating to a provider or using an abstract method, forcing a dependency on a low-level I/O detail."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/JsonFactory.java",
      "entity_name": "JsonFactory",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 32,
      "line_end": 527,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "High-level factory depends on concrete low-level async parser implementations",
      "reasoning": "JsonFactory imports and directly references concrete classes like NonBlockingByteArrayJsonParser and NonBlockingByteBufferJsonParser (implied by imports and methods L351, L362). As a high-level module, it should depend on the JsonParser abstraction rather than specific implementation details."
    }
  ]
}
```

findings.description: Direct instantiation of concrete parser implementation
findings.reasoning: The constructParser method directly instantiates UTF8StreamJsonParser (L299). This couples the bootstrapping logic to a specific parser implementation rather than depending on an abstraction or using a factory/injection mechanism.

findings.description: Abstract base class hard-codes dependency on concrete PrettyPrinter
findings.reasoning: The abstract class GeneratorBase directly instantiates DefaultPrettyPrinter (L378). Abstractions should not depend on details; hard-coding a specific implementation of an interface in a base class prevents users from easily substituting different defaults.

findings.description: Context object directly instantiates concrete buffer implementation
findings.reasoning: IOContext directly instantiates TextBuffer (L216) and ReadConstrainedTextBuffer (L220). This creates a tight coupling between the I/O context and specific buffer implementations, making it difficult to swap buffer management strategies.

findings.description: Base factory hard-codes dependency on concrete Writer implementation
findings.reasoning: TextualTSFactory, which serves as a high-level factory abstraction, directly instantiates UTF8Writer (L345) instead of delegating to a provider or using an abstract method, forcing a dependency on a low-level I/O detail.

findings.description: High-level factory depends on concrete low-level async parser implementations
findings.reasoning: JsonFactory imports and directly references concrete classes like NonBlockingByteArrayJsonParser and NonBlockingByteBufferJsonParser (implied by imports and methods L351, L362). As a high-level module, it should depend on the JsonParser abstraction rather than specific implementation details.
